_meta.title: M1 — the app-shell anchor (@agent-ui/app + ui-app-shell + isolation + a2ui-live re-host)
_meta.notes: PLAN mode (feeds the M1 build fan-out). Sliced by OWNING FILE: n1a=serial PREP (package base), n4a=serial INTEGRATION (shared measure-size.mjs). Each new gate (n1b layering, n2b/n2c browser, n4a size) ships a negative control anchored on a unique token. The behavior contract these tasks satisfy is the M1 SPEC (SPEC-R#); the implementation is the M1 LLD (LLD-C#).

nodes.label: M1 — the app-shell anchor: a new @agent-ui/app apex package + ui-app-shell layout primitive + opt-in isolation + the a2ui-live re-host
nodes.justify: grouping — the milestone root; its four subsystems are the child groups

nodes.label: package scaffold + layering boundary (SERIAL PREP — the fixed base every later slice builds against)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the new apex package and its no-cycle guarantee

nodes.label: @agent-ui/app package scaffold — packages/agent-ui/app/{package.json,tsconfig.json,src/index.ts}, workspace-wired, deps = {components, a2ui, shared}
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0 with packages/agent-ui/app/ resolving in the npm workspace; app/package.json `dependencies` == exactly {@agent-ui/components, @agent-ui/a2ui, @agent-ui/shared}; src/index.ts barrel present, tsc noEmit clean; no OTHER package's package.json is modified (git diff scoped to app/ + root workspace list)

nodes.label: import-layering trip-wire for the apex — app/src/layering.test.ts (app imports only {components, a2ui, shared, ./local}; nothing under components/ or a2ui/ imports @agent-ui/app)
nodes.accept: `npm test` exit 0 incl. app/src/layering.test.ts; a negative control anchored on a UNIQUE token (a planted upward/self import `@agent-ui/app` inside a2ui OR a components→a2ui edge) is grep-confirmed applied and proven to turn the test RED; a static grep proves no source under packages/agent-ui/components/src or /a2ui/src imports @agent-ui/app

nodes.label: the ui-app-shell layout primitive (the element)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the composed region/docking control + its gates + its isolation mode

nodes.label: ui-app-shell element build — controls/app-shell/{app-shell.ts,app-shell.css,app-shell.md}: region/docking model composing the shipped G9 layout family, typed literal-union props, single-file @scope CSS, descriptor
nodes.accept: `npm run check` exit 0; app-shell.ts extends UIElement (light-DOM default), region/docking props are typed literal unions via `static props`/ReactiveProps, and it composes ONLY the public @agent-ui/components barrel (grep: no `packages/**/src` deep import); app-shell.css declares only --ui-app-shell-* in a :where() block + consumes own+allowlisted shared tokens, lays the regions out, reflows on its OWN container width, and handles forced-colors; app-shell.md validates against the frontmatter schema and the contract↔props trip-wire is GREEN. NORMATIVE (ADR-0083/0084, ratified Kim 2026-07-06): ui-app-shell-region gains `landmark` (optional ARIA override, internals.role = landmark || REGION_ROLE[region] — `||` NOT `??`, the unset value is '' which nullish-coalescing misses) + `collapse:'hide'|'stack'` (default hide); the collapse=stack @container rule lands in BOTH the base app-shell.css AND the isolated :host mirror (n2c, keep-in-sync)

nodes.label: ui-app-shell gates — jsdom behaviour probes + cross-engine whole-shape/reflow browser smoke
nodes.accept: `npm test` exit 0 incl. app-shell.test.ts (jsdom: region/docking prop → DOM/attribute mapping; presence-driven region layout; empty-region + missing-region cases) AND `npm run test:browser` exit 0 incl. app-shell.browser.test.ts (Chromium AND WebKit: the whole region layout renders with non-zero bounding boxes; resizing the WRAPPER — not the viewport — reflows the regions; the forced-colors leg green); each assertion carries an anti-vacuous/biting check. NORMATIVE (ADR-0083/0084, ratified Kim 2026-07-06): jsdom asserts `landmark` override → internals.role = landmark || REGION_ROLE[region] (incl. out-of-set coercion); browser asserts a `collapse=stack` side region stays VISIBLE narrow while `collapse=hide` hides — biting NC = drop the stack rule, the region hides

nodes.label: ui-app-shell opt-in isolation (ADR-0082 mechanism: attachShadow + fleet-CSS-injected-in-shadow + the F1b :host grid variant) + browser validation, escalation-on-fail
nodes.accept: `npm run test:browser` exit 0 incl. the isolation legs (Chromium AND WebKit): with isolation ON, (a) a control composed inside the shell renders correct geometry+colour [tokens reach it via foundation-styles injected in-shadow; @scope-in-shadow + *-ramp re-derivation], (b) that control's own styles apply, (c) a host-page rule targeting the control does NOT change its computed style [no leak], AND (d) F1b — the region grid-area placement (R3) + narrow reflow (R5) hold under isolation via the injected :host grid variant, with a biting NC = regions land un-placed when the :host variant is omitted; default (isolation OFF) stays light-DOM byte-identical to n2a. The :host grid variant lives in THIS slice (app-shell-isolation.css / injected string), NOT the base app-shell.css (n2a UNCHANGED). IF any leg fails, an escalation note to Kim WITH the browser-truth evidence is filed and the decision (PRD-D3/ADR-0082) is NOT silently worked around

nodes.label: the reference app — a2ui-live re-hosted on the primitive (PRD-D5)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: the flagship proof that the primitive replaces bespoke chrome

nodes.label: re-express site/pages/a2ui-live on <ui-app-shell> — remove a2ui-live.css's OWN bespoke shell/layout CSS (shared _page.css untouched, deferred), prove net-negative chrome LOC
nodes.accept: `npm run check` (+ check:site) exit 0 AND `npm run build` exit 0 AND `npm test`+`npm run test:browser` green for the a2ui-live page; the bespoke shell/layout CSS in site/pages/a2ui-live.css is REMOVED; site/pages/_page.css is UNMODIFIED (`git diff` shows no change — the SHARED shell migration is deferred, not M1); `git diff --stat` shows net-negative bespoke-chrome LOC on a2ui-live's own chrome; the browser smoke shows the [chat | canvas] layout renders unchanged vs the pre-re-host snapshot AND no other site page regresses. NORMATIVE rework (ADR-0083/0084, ratified Kim 2026-07-06 — sequence AFTER n2a/n2b land the props): the composer region authors region=navigation landmark=complementary collapse=stack — its AX landmark reads complementary (not navigation) and it stays visible+interactive at a narrow container width (cross-engine)

nodes.label: integration + verification (SERIAL INTEGRATION + the reviewer gate)
nodes.justify: grouping — subsystem: shared-file wiring done once + the independent judgment gate

nodes.label: public barrel + size line-item — app/src/index.ts exports the shell; scripts/measure-size.mjs gains an @agent-ui/app line-item + budget; tree-shake proof
nodes.accept: app/src/index.ts exports the shell as the public entry; `npm run size` exit 0 carrying a NEW @agent-ui/app line-item measured vs the budget baseline recorded at M1 kickoff (provisional ceiling ≤ ~3 KB gz marginal); a tree-shake probe proves importing the shell drags only it + the composed real deps (no unrelated fleet control); the measure-size.mjs edit is the ONLY writer of that shared file this milestone (single-writer integration slice); a planted over-budget number proves the gate bites

nodes.label: independent component-reviewer pass on ui-app-shell (pre-commit NO-GO gate)
nodes.accept: component-reviewer verdict recorded GO on <ui-app-shell> (zero blockers; COMPOSE ≥4 AND REALIZE ≥4) BEFORE the M1 commit; any NO-GO finding is closed and delta-re-reviewed to GO

actions.label: create a new package that depends on exactly {components, a2ui, shared} — the apex of the DAG
guarantee no dependency cycle exists and nothing inward imports the apex
lay out the persistent application regions from one composed primitive
dock a surface into a named region
reflow the layout responsively to its own container width (intrinsic, no breakpoint props)
optionally isolate the shell's styles from a host page (opt-in)
keep theme tokens reaching controls composed inside an isolated shell
keep host-page CSS from leaking into an isolated shell
survive forced-colors: active (shell chrome + composed controls)
assemble the a2ui-live chrome from the primitive with zero bespoke shell/layout CSS
expose the shell through the package's public barrel (consumable, tree-shakeable)
measure + budget the package's marginal size as a size-gate line-item
describe the shell's public surface in a {name}.md descriptor + pass the contract↔props trip-wire
judge the shell against COMPOSE/REALIZE ≥4 before commit (the reviewer gate)
escalate to Kim with evidence if the isolation validation fails any leg
prove the region layout + container reflow cross-engine, whole-shape (not per-part)

edges.why: the shell element lives in the package n1a scaffolds
the layering test asserts the import graph of the package n1a creates
the gates exercise the element n2a builds
the isolation mode extends the element n2a builds
the a2ui-live re-host composes the ui-app-shell element n2a builds
the size line-item measures the element's final byte cost including n2c's isolation code, and the barrel exports the finished element
the reviewer judges the element only after its behaviour gates are green
the reviewer judges the element only after its isolation mode is resolved
